Title: The Newest Instagram "Exploit" is the Goofiest I've Seen
Description: Yesterday, a slew of Instagram accounts, including some high profile ones like the Obama White House account, seemingly got hacked. I've seen my share of exploits and takeover techniques, but this is the most unserious, "almost too stupid to be true" of them all.

Title: Weird projects I shipped with AI
Description: Where are all the AI-generated projects? This is a common question from AI skeptics: if LLMs are so good at writing code, where is the tsunami of new AI-generated apps, services and games?

Title: PrismML — Introducing 1-bit and Ternary Bonsai Image 4B: Image Generation for Local Devices
Converting ISO Country Codes to Flag Emojis
Description: Did you know that there's a simple formula you can use to convert from an ISO two-letter country code to the emoji of the flag of that country. I've made an interactive thingy to demonstrate it, and shared some other fun things I've learned while playing around with ISO 3166 and flag emoji.

Title: Cheap software won't make engineering cheap
Description: John Wang's personal website

Title: Simple systems are the best systems
Description:
When it comes to systems design, John Gall knew his stuff. He even has a LAW1 about it:

A complex system that works is invariably found to have evolved from a simple system that worked. A complex system designed from scratch never works and cannot be patched up to make it work. You have to start over with a working simple system.

Gall isn’t the only promoter of simple systems. Here’s Dijkstra (maybe you’ve heard of him?)

Title: Colonies on Mars require more than engineering
Description: Elon Musk talks about a city on Mars the way a developer talks about a new subdivision outside Phoenix. The engineering problems are hard, but they are the kind of hard we know how to attack: get there cheaply, make propellant on site, close the life-support loop, shield against the radiation, and eventually warm the place up. There is a technical roadmap. There is a financial roadmap. There is no social roadmap at all.

Title: Encrypted Client Hello: A Big Tech Privacy Fix
Description: Much of the traffic on the web is now encrypted thanks to SSL/TLS (https). This prevents attackers from seeing the contents of your traffic. But they can still see which domains you are connecting to, and that might be a problem still.

Title: Snapshot-based CMEK Bypass: Cross-Project Disk Replication
Description: This follow-up demonstrates a snapshot-based bypass that complements the disk-cloning attack described earlier.
